characteristics.$text: Once per battle, when you have to remove a Cult Ambush marker because your opponent has moved too close to it, if one or more models from your army with this ability are on the battlefield, you can use this ability. If you do, instead of removing that marker, you can place it anywhere on the battlefield that is within 12" of a model from your army with this ability and more than 8" horizontally away from all enemy units (if this is not possible, this ability is not considered to have been used and that marker is removed as normal).

This model can be attached to the following units:
- **^^Acolyte Hybrids^^**
- **^^Hybrid Metamorphs^^**
- **^^Neophyte Hybrids^^**

characteristics.$text: Friendly **^^Genestealer Cult Infantry^^** models can be set up or end any type of move on top of the floor sections of this **^^Fortification^^**.

characteristics.name: Description
characteristics.$text: Each time one of your Cult Ambush markers is removed as the result of an enemy unit ending any kind of move within 8" of it, you can instead move that marker so that it is wholly within 8" of this **^^Fortification^^** and more than 8" horizontally away from all enemy units (if this is not possible, that marker is removed as normal).

Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this FORTIFICATION, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text:
While an enemy unit is only within Engagement Range of one or more FORTIFICATION from your army:
■ That unit can still be selected as the target of ranged attacks, but each time such an attack is made, unless it is made with a Pistol, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.
■ Models in that unit do not need to take Desperate Escape tests due to Falling Back while Battle-shocked, except for those that will move over enemy models when doing so.

characteristics.$text: Improve the Leadership characteristic of models in the bearer's unit by 2. Each time the bearer's unit fails a Leadership test, the bearer suffers 1 mortal wound.

characteristics.$text: Each time you select the bearer's unit as the target of a Battle Tactic Stratagem, you can select one other unit within 12" of the bearer's unit that is also an eligible target for that Stratagem; that selected unit is also affected by that Stratagem.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: While an enemy **^^Battleline^^** unit is within 9" of the bearer's unit, worsen the Leadership characteristic of models in that enemy unit by 1, and subtract 1 from the Objective Control characteristic of models in that enemy unit (to a minimum of 0).
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Acolyte Iconward^^** model only. While a friendly **^^Genestealer Cults Infantry^^** or **^^Genestealer Cults Mounted^^** unit is within 6" of the bearer, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of melee weapons equipped by models in that unit.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Improve the Armour Penetration and Damage characteristics of melee weapons equipped by the bearer by 1.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text:
**^^Infantry^^** model (excluding **^^Patriarch^^** models) only.
Add 3" to the bearer's Move characteristic, and the bearer gains the following ability:
**Pervasion Veil:** Once per battle, at the end of any phase, provided this model is not leading a unit, it can use this ability. If it does, remove it from the battlefield and set it up again anywhere on the battlefield more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy units.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text:
- Subtract 1 from the Toughness characteristic of this model.
- Worsen the Weapon Skill and Ballistic Skill characteristics of weapons equipped by this model by 1.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time you allocate an attack to a model in this model's unit, you must allocate it to this model.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: You cannot target this model's unit with Stratagems other than Core Stratagems.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text:
- This model cannot be attached to a unit that has another model attached to it, and no other models can be attached to a unit this model is attached to.
- At the start of the Fight phase, if this model's unit is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and within 9" of one or more other friendly **^^Genestealer Cults Character^^** units that are also within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, this model's unit is not eligible to fight until after those other friendly **^^Genestealer Cults Character^^** units that are eligible to fight have done so.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time you target this model's unit with a Stratagem, roll one D6; on a 4+, until the end of the turn, you cannot target this model's unit with that Stratagem (do not spend any CP).
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: In your Charge phase, if this model's unit is eligible to declare a charge and is within 7" of one or more enemy units, you must declare a charge with this model's unit.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: After each Crusade battle, you can ignore your first failed Out of Action test.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: When a unit from your Crusade force gains the Weapons Modifications Battle Honour, you can roll 3D6 (instead of 2D6) to randomly determine three Weapon Modifications for the weapon to gain.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time you add a **^^Vehicle^^** unit to your Order of Battle, that unit gains 6XP (and therefore gains the Blooded Rank); select one Battle Honour for it as normal.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: After each Crusade battle, one one D6: on a 4+, you gain 1 additional Requisition point.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time you select a unit to be Marked for Greatness, that unit gains 1 additional XP.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: After each Crusade battle, each **^^Battleline^^** unit that was part of your Crusade army and survived the battle gains 1XP.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text:
In your Shooting phase, each time you select a **^^Crossfire^^** unit to shoot, you can select up to two other **^^Crossfire^^** units from your Crusade army that are eligible to shoot to perform a Deadly Crossfire, Then, you can select one enemy unit that would be an eligible target for one or more ranged weapons equipped by models in each of the selected **^^Crossfire^^** units. Until the end of the turn, that enemy unit is a Crossfire target.

Each time one of those selected **^^Crossfire^^** units makes a ranged attack, models in that unit can only target their Crossfire target, and:
- If two **^^Crossfire^^** units were selected to perform a Deadly Crossfire against the at enemy unit, add 1 to the Hit roll.
- If three **^^Crossfire^^** units were selected to perform a Deadly Crossfire against that enemy unit, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: You gain 6 Planning points, which you can spend to purchase Shrewd Strategy abilities to use during that battle.

In your Shooting phase, after his unit has shot, select one enemy **^^Infantry^^** unit hit by one or more of those attacks that was a Crossfire target. Until the start of your next turn, that enemy unit is suppressed. While a unit is suppressed, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.

characteristics.$text: Once per battle, at the start of the Command phase, if this unit is within range of one objective market you control, you can sabotage it. If you do, place this unit's Booby Trap token onto that objective marker (an objective marker cannot have more than one of these tokens on it at a time). While an objective marker has one of your Booby Trap tokens on it, it is Sabotaged, and each time an enemy unit ends a Normal, Advance, Fall Back or Charge move within range of that objective marker, roll one D6: on a 2+, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds, If, at the end of any phase or turn, that objective marker is under your opponent's control, remove your Booby Trap token from it.

Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets a Crossfire target, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

rules.description:
During the Select Crusade Blessings step, you can spend your Planning points to purchase one or more Shrewd Strategy abilities. All Planning points unspent at the end of this step are lost. Each time you purchase one of these abilities, select one **^^Genestealer Cults^^** unit in your Crusade army; until the end of the battle, that unit has that ability and the first time it is set up on the battlefield, place the relevant Shrewd Strategy ability token next to it to remind you (and your opponent) that you have upgraded that unit that battle. You can purchase the same Shrewd Strategy ability multiple times, but each unit cannot have more than one Shrewd Strategy ability per battle.

Note that if a unit with a Shrewd Strategy ability is destroyed during the battle and a new identical unit is added to your Crusade army as a result of the Cult Ambush ability, that unit does not have the Shrewd Strategy abilities that the original unit had.
rules.name: Shrewd Strategy Abilities

characteristics.$text: Each time a unit that is beloved of the cult gains 1 or more XP in a Crusade battle and survives that battle, after that battle, roll one D6 for each other unit from your Crusade army that survived that battle: on a 6, the unit you rolled for gains 1XP.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: This unit is eligible to shoot in a turn in which it Fell Back. Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets its Crossfire target, if this unit made a Fall Back move this turn, that attack has the **[LETHAL HITS]** ability.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: You can target this unit with the Rapid Ingress Stratagem for 0CP.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Enemy units cannot the Fire Overwatch Stratagem to shoot at this unit.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time you purchase a Shrewd Strategy ability, if that upgrade is given to this unit, reduce the Planning Points needed to purchase it by 1 (to a minimum of 1). In addition, at the Upgrade Crusade Cards step, if this unit would gain XP from Dealers of Death, it gains 1 additional XP.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets its Crossfire target, that attack has the **[SUSTAINED HITS 1]** ability.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: Ranged weapons equipped by models in this unit have the **[ASSAULT]** ability.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Jackal Alphus^^** or **^^Sanctus^^** model only. Select one ranged weapon equipped by the bearer (excluding a weapon that has been replaced by an Enhancement or Crsade Relic, or upgraded with any Battle Traits). That weapon is now a Crusade Relic; not this on the bearer's Crusade card. That weapon gains the **[ANTI-CHARACTER 4+]** and **[DEVASTATING WOUNDS]** abilities.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Reducus Saboteur^^** model only. If your Crusade army includes Shrewd Strategy abilities, then the bearer can be given the Booby Traps Shrewd Strategy ability for 0 Planning points. In addition, when the bearer sabotages an objective marker, each time an enemy unit ends a Normal, Advance, Fall Back or Charge move within range of that objective marker, roll one D6: on a 2+ that enemy unit suffers D3+3 mortal wounds, instead of D3.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Clamavus^^** model only. Each time an enemy unit takes a battle-shock test as a result of the bearer's Voice of New Truths ability, subtract 1 from that test and, if that test is failed, that enemy unit suffers 3 mortal wounds.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Primus^^** model only. If the bearer is select to be your **^^Strategist^^**, you gain an additional 3 Planning points to spend on Shrewd Strategy abilities. Any Planning points not spent by the end of the Select Crusade Blessings step are not lost - instead, each time a new **^^Genestealer Cults^^** unit is added to your Crusade army via the Cult Ambush rule, you can spend any remaining Planning points to purchase a Shrewd Strategy ability for that new unit before it is set up on the battlefield. Any Planning points not spent by the end of the battle are lost.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Primus^^** or **^^Locus^^** model only. Select one weapon equipped by the bearer (excluding a weapon that has been replaced by an Enhancement or Crusade Relic, or upgraded with any Battle Traits). That weapon is now a Crusade Relic; note  this on the bearer's Crusade card. That weapon has the **[LETHAL HITS]** ability, and each time the bearer makes an attack with that weapon, you can re-roll the Hit roll.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Patriarch^^** or **^^Magus^^** model only. The bearer can use its Psychic Familiar ability one additional time during the battle (this must be in different turns).
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Acolyte Iconward^^** model only. Add 2 to the bearer's Objective Control characteristic. In addition, when you use the Summon the Cult ability, you can place the Cult Ambush marker anywhere on the battlefield that is within 18" of the bearer and more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy units (if this is not possible, the Summon the Cult ability is not considered to have been used and that marker is removed as normal).
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Nexos^^** model only. Each time the bearer uses its Cult Infiltration ability, you can select up to two of your Cult Ambush markers that are on the battlefield instead of selecting one; each marker selected that has not been moved this turn can be moved up to 6".
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Benefictus^^** model only. The bearer's ranged Psychic weapons have the **[DEVASTATING WOUNDS]** ability and the Range characteristics of those weapons is increased by 6".
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Kelermorph^^** model only. Once per battle, in your Shooting phase, after this unit has shot, select one enemy unit (excluding **^^Vehicles^^**) hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the battle, that enemy unit is poisoned. At the start of each Command phase, roll one D6 for each poisoned unit on the battlefield: on a 4+, that unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Biophagus^^** model only. The bearer can use their Alchemicus Familiar ability one additional time during the battle (this must be in different turns).
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: **^^Abominant^^** model only. While the bearer is leading a unit, each time you roll a D6 for its The Chosen One ability, add 1 to the result.
characteristics.name: Description

sharedRules.description:
If your Army Faction is **GENESTEALER CULTS**, you start the battle with a number of Resurgence points, depending on the battle size, as shown below.

- Incursion: 6 Resurgence points
- Strike Force: 10 Resurgence points
- Onslaught: 14 Resurgence points

Each time a unit from your army is destroyed, if every model in that unit has this ability you can spend the relevant number of Resurgence points shown below based on that unit's Starting Strength.

**---Aberrants---**
5 models = 4 Resurgence Points
10 models = 8 Resurgence Points

**---Acolyte Hybrids with Autopistols, Acolyte Hybrids with Hand Flamers, Hybrid Metamorphs---**
5 models = 2 Resurgence Points
10 models = 4 Resurgence Points

**---Atalan Jackals---**
5 models = 2 Resurgence Points
10 models = 6 Resurgence Points

**---Neophyte Hybrids---**
10 models = 3 Resurgence Points
20 models = 6 Resurgence Points

**---Purestrain Genestealers---**
5 models = 2 Resurgence Points
10 models = 6 Resurgence Points

If you do:
- Add a new unit to your army identical to your destroyed unit, in Cult Ambush, at its Starting Strength, with all of its wounds remaining and any **[ONE SHOT]** weapons those models are equipped with considered as not having been shot.
- Place one Cult Ambush marker (see below) anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy units (if this is not possible, no marker is placed).

**UNITS IN CULT AMBUSH**
Cult Ambush is a type of Strategic Reserves. Units in Cult Ambush cannot be targeted with the Rapid Ingress Stratagem, but can be set up on the battlefield using a Cult Ambush marker as described below, or by following the rules for Strategic Reserves in a subsequent turn. Units in Cult Ambush are not automically destroyed at the end of the third battle round.

**CULT AMBUSH MARKERS**
Use a circular 32mm diameter marker for Cult Ambush markers. If an enemy model (excluding **AIRCRAFT**) ends any kind of move within 8" of a Cult Ambush marker you placed, that Cult Ambush marker is removed from the battlefield. At the end of your opponent’s next Movement phase, for each of your Cult Ambush markers still on the battlefield, you can select one unit from your army that is in Cult Ambush to make an **ingress move**. For that **ingress move**, set that unit up with at least one model in that unit in base contact with that Cult Ambush marker and all other models placed wholly within 3" of that Cult Ambush marker (that Cult Ambush marker is then removed from the battlefield). Units in Cult Ambush can make an **ingress move** in the first battle round using this rule.
sharedRules.name: Cult Ambush
